notes: User runs turn 1 on model A (gpt-5.5, the config default), invokes /model mid-session to switch to model B (gpt-5.3-codex), then runs turn 2 on model B — all on ONE live codex rollout session. The daemon sees proc-PID pre-session born ready, then the rollout session reconcile (new session_id) born ready, then two working→ready arcs on the SAME rollout session_id, then process_exited at REPL teardown. The /model switch is an interactive two-step picker (codex 0.130.0): the driver opens it, navigates to gpt-5.3-codex, confirms, then confirms the reasoning-level sub-screen — no extra session is minted (same session_id throughout). The model-name TRANSITION itself (A→B) is NOT expressible in the expected.jsonl phase DSL: the validator Phase struct (validate/expected.go) asserts only state/kind on the events.jsonl lifecycle stream, which carries no model_name field. The model change is captured at metrics level in summary.model_name of the .replay.json.golden (final value = model B = gpt-5.3-codex), which replay-fixtures.sh diffs. Codex carries the active model on per-turn turn_context.payload.model; the parser sets ev.ModelName there (Skip=true) and the tailer applies it via applyMetadata even for skipped events, so each turn updates metrics.ModelName. expected.jsonl therefore asserts the lifecycle skeleton; the golden asserts the switch landed.

text: Pre-session appears in ready within 1s of launch
Rollout session reconciles (different session_id), born ready
PID bound to the rollout session
Turn 1 prompt (model A) → working on the rollout session
Turn 1 ends → back to ready; ModelName = model A (gpt-5.5)
After /model switch to model B, turn 2 prompt → working on the SAME session
Turn 2 ends in ready on model B (gpt-5.3-codex); session never entered waiting; TotalTokens accumulates across the switch
Driver tears down the REPL — process_exited on the rollout session
